nodes.title: Sampler Preview
widgets_values:
Tiny VAE for sampler previews. 
Optionally you can drop this for a lower res latent RGB built into the node 

https://github.com/madebyollin/taehv/blob/main/safetensors/taeltx2_3.safetensors

## Prompt Relay — per-segment text prompts.
Instead of one prompt describing the whole clip, you write "0–30f: person walking, 30–60f: sits down, 60–120f: waves" and each prompt only conditions its slice of the video.
## Guide keyframes:
Image segments on the timeline become first/middle/last-frame guides at whatever frame position and strength you set them to.
## Audio:
Optional audio clips you can drop on the timeline, trim, and overlap; node returns a single combined waveform (and an audio latent if you connect an Audio VAE for LTXV-audio variants).

nodes.title: Prompting LTX-2
widgets_values:
## LTX-2 Prompting Tips
1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time  
2. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene  
3. **Reference Image**: Do not repeat details already present  
4. **Consistency**: Avoid instructions that do not match the reference image, as this will degrade results

nodes.title: About  Models
widgets_values:
Download models from here:


https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy

Text encoder : https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2

nodes.title: User Made Loras
widgets_values: If using some user made LTX-2 loras they sometimes are not trained on audio, so it will produce very noisy audio outputs. Try use KJNodes LTX-2 Lora Loader Advanced in such cases, and set the non video strenght to zero

nodes.title: FAQ
widgets_values:
# FAQ

**How do I use this??**

I'll be posting tutorials on my YouTube channel soon!
<https://www.youtube.com/@WhatDreamsCost/>

**Where do I download these models??**

There is a good list of models and download links here: <https://github.com/wildminder/awesome-ltx2?tab=readme-ov-file>

Also the latest v1.1 spatial upscaler can be found here: <https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/tree/main>

The tiny vae (taeltx2_3.safetensors) is found here:
<https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/vae/taeltx2_3.safetensors>

**I'm getting errors when running this workflow!**

Double check that you have all of the correct models loaded. This is using the newest version of 2x upscaler (v1.1). You can change it to the older one under the Model Loader.

**How can I support you?**

Help me get a job. I'm unemployed and literally broke. (seriously, i'll even scrub toilets for minimum wage)

Or follow me on my socials :)

<https://x.com/WhatDreamsCost>

<https://www.instagram.com/whatdreamscost/>

<https://www.youtube.com/@WhatDreamsCost/>

nodes.title: About Size
widgets_values:
Width & height settings must be divisible by 32 + 1. 
Frame count must be divisible by 8 + 1. 


Running with invalid parameters **will not cause errors**. Instead, the flow will silently choose the closest valid parameters. 

By default, we are using 720p resolution. You can try 1920*1088 if you have a powerful GPU.

nodes.title: Video settings
widgets_values:
If low on Ram/Vram, try width height to 832 x 480 or 960 x 544.  If you can, run at 1280 x 720 or higher.


Length in seconds :  try 5, 10 or 20. 
fps : 24 or 25 (or 48 or 50 if your pc can run it)

# Structure

- LTX-Comfy (working folder name)

  - custom_name_EXR
    - v001
    - v002
    - ....

  - custom_name_h264.0001.mov
  - custom_name_proRes.0001.mov

nodes.title: OVERLAY - FEEDBACK
widgets_values:
Base animation - reference 



Motion: 1/5

Sync: 0/5

Sharp: 4/5
A brown grizzly bear, speaks to the camera. Forest backdrop.
A brown grizzly bear faces the camera and says "{text}". The bear clearly forms the words with its mouth, jaw, and lips.
The brown grizzly bear pauses between spoken words. Its mouth closes naturally while it keeps facing the camera.
